events.summary: City Council approved 10-0 a home rule petition authorizing Salem to increase the local lodging excise on hotels, motels, bed-and-breakfasts, and short-term rentals from the current 6% cap to up to 8.75%. Revenue from the additional 2.75-percentage-point increment would be held in a separate special fund dedicated to school and municipal facility capital projects.

events.project_name: South Essex Sewerage District Centennial Plan — Wastewater Infrastructure Legislation
events.address: South Essex Sewerage District, Salem, MA
events.summary: City Council referred to Committee of the Whole a resolution supporting SESD's filing of state legislation to address aging wastewater infrastructure and rising operating costs. The proposed legislation would authorize the Centennial Plan with a $390 million cost cap (Tiers 1 and 2), allow bonding and borrowing to finance the work, establish a non-exempt FY2028 budget ceiling of $30.5 million with 2.5% annual escalation, and exempt Centennial Plan construction from Proposition 2½ limits; without relief SESD warns of permit violations and potential EPA consent order affecting Salem Sound.

events.project_name: National Grid Utility Pole Installation and Relocation — Beach Avenue and Sutton Avenue
events.address: Beach Avenue and Sutton Avenue, Salem, MA
events.summary: City Council ordered a public hearing for June 11, 2026 on National Grid's petition to install one new singly-owned utility pole on Beach Avenue and relocate one existing singly-owned pole on Sutton Avenue.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences (Phase 2)
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.applicant: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC
events.summary: Continuation of public hearing for WinnDevelopment's up-to-145-unit affordable residential project proposing adaptive reuse of three former Salem State University South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus one new four-story building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces and landscaped common areas. Key open issues include resolution of stormwater peer-review comments, MassDOT access-permit stormwater requirements (including a contested detention basin that would require tree removal on Route 1A), and outstanding architectural and traffic submittals. Board voted 7-0 to continue to June 25, 2026.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.project_name: Tabernacle Congregational Church Redevelopment
events.summary: Continuation of public hearing for Beacon Communities' proposal to renovate the existing Tabernacle Congregational Church and add two new building additions within current parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans, while maintaining existing church uses; the project includes 9 parking spaces and site improvements. Discussion focused on two dimensional peer-review findings: a required two-foot pervious strip between parking line and pervious surface, and curb-cut totals across frontage on Washington, Federal, and Lynde Streets (the Lynde Street cut to be removed). Board voted 7-0 to continue to June 4, 2026 pending peer-review response.

events.project_name: 2 Bridge Street Restaurant
events.summary: The ZBA approved parking variances for 2 Bridge, LLC to operate a 99-seat restaurant (52 inside, 42 outside seats plus 5 staff) on the first floor of an existing waterfront building in the B2/Entrance Corridor Overlay District. The variances allow 12–18 on-site parking spaces where 28 are required, including undersized spaces abutting the property line; a special condition requires the petitioner to work with the Transportation Department to identify and sign compact-car spaces per the April 24, 2026 Griffin Engineering Group parking plan.

events.project_name: House of Seven Gables Climate Adaptation Plan — Phase 1A Counting House Relocation
events.address: 115 Derby Street, Salem, MA (Map 41, Lot 0300; Map 41, Lot 0303)
events.applicant: House of Seven Gables Settlement Association
events.summary: The ZBA approved a parking finding for the House of Seven Gables Settlement Association's Phase 1A climate adaptation plan, which relocates a small historic counting house from a vulnerable harbor-edge position to a more protected area on the property. The relocation displaces nine parking spaces; the Board found the reconfigured lot still satisfies ordinance requirements for employee and visitor parking at this museum-classified property.

contacts.name: House of the Seven Gables Settlement Association

events.project_name: 33 School Street ADU — Attic Conversion
events.summary: The ZBA approved a finding for 33 School Street LLC to convert the sloped-roof attic of a nonconforming two-family structure into a 647-square-foot accessory dwelling unit, creating a third unit in the R2 district. The project involves no change to the building's footprint or exterior massing.

events.project_name: 34 Shore Avenue ADU Above Garage
events.summary: The ZBA approved a variance and ADU finding for Jacob Levine to construct a second-story addition above an existing CMU garage, resulting in a 2.5-story, 24-foot-tall accessory dwelling unit sited at zero feet from the nonconforming lot's side and rear property lines in the R1/Coastal Resiliency Overlay District. Special conditions require removal of windows facing the abutting property and addition of a minimum six-foot privacy screen along the eastern setback.

events.project_name: Salem State South Campus Multi-Family Residential Development
events.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Rd, Salem, MA (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327)
events.summary: Continued public hearing on a Notice of Intent for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State University South Campus site. Unit count not stated in the agenda.

events.project_name: Brooks Street Drainage Rehabilitation
events.address: Brooks Street between Jefferson Ave and Lawrence St, Salem, MA (Map 24)
events.summary: Public hearing on a Request for Determination of Applicability for rehabilitation of existing drainage on Brooks Street, including replacement of existing drainage features and installation of new features within a wetlands resource area.

events.project_name: Bay View Ave Beach Seawall and Sidewalk Improvements
events.address: Bay View Ave Beach, Salem, MA (Map 45)
events.summary: Public hearing on a Request for Determination of Applicability for replacement of the sidewalk along Bay View Ave, replacement of the stone beach access ramp with revetment stones, and addition of rip-rap stone to the back of the existing seawall to deter erosion at Bay View Ave Beach.

events.project_name: House of the Seven Gables Floodproofing and Site Improvements
events.applicant: House of the Seven Gables Settlement Association

applicant_reps.name: Paul Wright
applicant_reps.firm: House of the Seven Gables Settlement Association

events.summary: Public hearing on a Notice of Intent for relocation of the Counting House and a boat play structure to inland locations, installation of floodproofing elements, and restoration and realignment of visitor pathways and landscaping at 115 Derby St within a wetlands resource area.

contacts.name: House of the Seven Gables Settlement Association

events.project_name: FY2027 General Fund Capital Improvement Program Bond Order
events.summary: City Council referred a $7,862,000 bond order to the Committee on Administration and Finance for the FY2027 General Fund CIP. Largest allocations include $1,600,000 to cap the transfer station, $900,000 for citywide road, sidewalk, and crosswalk improvements, $810,000 for school technology, $700,000 for school security and life safety infrastructure, $600,000 for the DPS Sidewalk Implementation Project, $600,000 for school MEP and HVAC, $500,000 for Winter Island building stabilization, and $400,000 for Forest River Park main bathrooms.

events.project_name: FY2027 Water and Sewer Capital Improvement Program Bond Order
events.summary: City Council referred a $7,660,000 bond order for FY2027 water and sewer infrastructure upgrades throughout Salem. Key projects include $1,300,000 for Jefferson Avenue (Laurent to Ocean) and Ocean Avenue utility repairs, $1,200,000 for water transmission main system improvements, $1,000,000 to relocate the Willow Avenue offshore sewer pipe, $1,000,000 for Canal Street water main work (Forest Avenue to Washington Street), $825,000 for water distribution main improvements, and $800,000 for Bridge Street drainage (Lynn to North).

events.project_name: FY2027 Short-Term Capital Outlay Appropriation
events.summary: City Council referred a $5,723,341 short-term capital appropriation covering more than 60 discrete FY2027 projects across departments. Notable items include $250,000 for a Master Plan update, $207,822 for terminal building design phase, $200,000 each for Pioneer Village renovation and Salem Common Bandstand, $200,000 for Salem Police Headquarters general repairs and upgrades, $156,333 for anti-vehicle bollards at Salem Common, and multiple road design, school, library, parks, and transportation projects.

events.project_name: FY2027 Water/Sewer Fund Retained Earnings Capital Projects
events.summary: City Council referred a $1,080,000 appropriation from water/sewer retained earnings for specific utility repair projects: $650,000 for Chestnut Street utility repairs from Summer to Cambridge Streets, $200,000 for water infrastructure repair and replacement, $130,000 for Winthrop Street sewer repairs, and $100,000 for sewer infrastructure repair.

events.project_name: Horace Mann Laboratory School Accelerated Repair — Decarbonization and Electrification
events.summary: City Council adopted a $250,000 appropriation for feasibility study and schematic design of an accelerated repair project at Horace Mann Laboratory School, 79 Willson Street. The described scope includes potential roof replacement or modification, replacement of HVAC equipment and distribution systems, and installation of a geoexchange heat pump system and photovoltaic solar panels to decarbonize and electrify the building. The City has applied for an MSBA grant; any project costs exceeding the grant remain the City's responsibility.

events.project_name: National Grid Conduit Installation — Lafayette Street & College Drive
events.address: Lafayette Street & College Drive, Salem, MA
events.summary: City Council ordered a public hearing for May 28, 2026 on a National Grid petition to install electrical conduits on Lafayette Street and College Drive to provide service to 352 Lafayette Street.

events.project_name: FY27 Water and Sewer Capital Improvement Program - Bond Authorization
events.summary: The City Council referred a $7,660,000 bond authorization for FY27 water and sewer capital improvements to the Committee on Admin & Finance. Projects include Willow Avenue sewer pipe relocation ($1M), Canal Street water main improvements ($1M), Bridge Street drainage ($800K), Jefferson Avenue and Ocean Avenue utility repairs ($1.3M), water transmission main system improvements ($1.2M), citywide sewer infiltration/inflow elimination ($610K), and citywide drain/sewer cleaning and inspection ($525K).

events.project_name: FY27 Water and Sewer CIP - Retained Earnings Projects
events.summary: The City Council referred a $1,080,000 appropriation from Water/Sewer Fund Retained Earnings for utility repairs at specific locations: Chestnut Street sewer and utility repairs from Summer Street to Cambridge Street ($650K), Winthrop Street sewer work ($130K), sewer infrastructure repair ($100K), and water infrastructure repair and replacement ($200K).

events.project_name: FY27 General Fund Capital Improvement Program - Bond Authorization
events.summary: The City Council referred a $7,862,000 bond authorization for FY27 general fund capital improvements. Largest items include Transfer Station capping ($1.6M), citywide roads/sidewalks/crosswalks ($900K), school technology ($810K), school MEP and HVAC ($600K), DPS Sidewalk Implementation Project ($600K), school security and life safety infrastructure ($700K), Salem Street sidewalk and crosswalk improvements ($500K), Winter Island building stabilization ($500K), Forest River Park main bathroom ($400K), school playgrounds ($400K), High Street Park renovation ($325K), and Jefferson/Jackson intersection design ($227K).

events.project_name: FY27 Road Corridor Design Studies
events.summary: The FY27 budget funds design studies for four named road corridors totaling $680,000: Jefferson/Jackson intersection design ($227K from bond authorization), Willson Street design ($220K), Proctor Street Corridor from Mansell to Highland ($154K), and Proctor/Pope/Mansell intersection design ($79K). These design phases precede future street reconstruction and are funded across the bond authorization and Capital Outlay Fund.

events.project_name: Salem Master Plan Update
events.summary: The FY27 Capital Outlay Fund includes $250,000 for a Master Plan Update under the Planning department. No scope detail or timeline beyond the appropriation is provided in the agenda.

events.project_name: Winter Island Building Stabilization
events.summary: The FY27 budget includes $560,000 for stabilization of the former Coast Guard structures at Winter Island — $500,000 through the General Fund bond authorization (Planning department) and $60,000 through the Capital Outlay Fund. The mayor's budget letter cites $660,000 for this project, suggesting possible additional funding beyond the two appropriation orders.

events.project_name: National Grid Conduit Installation - Lafayette Street and College Drive
events.address: Lafayette St and College Drive, Salem, MA (service point: 352 Lafayette St)
events.summary: National Grid petitioned the City Council for permission to install conduits on Lafayette Street and College Drive to provide electrical service to 352 Lafayette Street. A public hearing was ordered for May 28, 2026.

events.project_name: Szetela Lane Phase 3 DEP Environmental Cleanup
events.summary: The FY27 Capital Outlay Fund includes $200,000 for Phase 3 of environmental remediation at Szetela Lane, managed by the Public Services department in accordance with DEP requirements. This is the third identified phase of the cleanup effort.

events.project_name: 170 North Street Residential
events.applicant: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC
events.summary: Planning Board approved Site Plan Review for 8 residential units within an existing building at 170 North Street, along with 16 parking spaces at the rear including an existing detached garage. Property is in the R2 and Entrance Corridor Overlay zoning districts; applicant is bringing the building into compliance following prior permitting difficulties under the previous owner.

contacts.name: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

events.project_name: 4 Franklin Street Site Plan Amendment
events.summary: Planning Board approved an amendment to the 2021 Site Plan Review and Special Permits for 4 Franklin Street, modifying the building to reduce office space from two stories to one story and extend the ambulance garage. Site revisions include updated parking layout, lighting, landscaping, and utilities; a second phase on the North River side of the site remains for future redevelopment.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences Phase 2
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Avenue, and 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing for WinnDevelopment's up to 145-unit Forest River Residences Phase 2, proposing adaptive reuse of three former Salem State University South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus a new four-story residential building along Harrison Road. The project includes approximately 159 off-street surface parking spaces and landscaped pedestrian connections; continued to May 21, 2026, pending engineering peer review and Design Review Board approval of plans.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.project_name: 25 Bow Street / 32 Ord Street Lot Split
events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA
events.summary: The ZBA approved a variance and special permit allowing 25 Bow Salem, LLC to subdivide an existing R1 lot into two parcels — a 5,008-sq-ft lot along Bow Street (retaining the existing home) and a 6,909-sq-ft lot along Ord Street (for a new single-family dwelling) — both receiving dimensional relief for frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. A second-story deck on the Bow Street parcel was also approved, subject to deed-recorded privacy fencing and arborvitae conditions negotiated with abutters.

events.project_name: CarFive Motors Use Expansion — 296 Highland Avenue
events.summary: The ZBA approved a special permit allowing CarFive Motors LLC to add motor vehicle general repair and bodywork use to an existing automobile dealership at 296 Highland Avenue (B2 zone, Entrance Corridor Overlay District), with no change to the building footprint or parking lot. Special conditions require reduction of the southern curb cut per ordinance and downward-facing lights on fixtures adjacent to residential properties.

events.project_name: 97 Boston Street Pet Grooming and Daycare Use Change
events.summary: The ZBA denied a special permit that would have converted a nonconforming medical training and certification use at 97 Boston Street (R2 zone, Entrance Corridor Overlay District) to a pet grooming and daycare business with a fenced outdoor pet playground at the rear. The board cited absent site plans, parking deficiencies, and noise incompatibility with the surrounding dense residential neighborhood; five abutters testified in opposition and the motion failed 0–5.

events.project_name: 3A & 5 Winter Island Road ANR Endorsement
events.address: 3A Winter Island Road / 5 Winter Island Road, Salem, MA
events.summary: Peter Haywood received 9-0 endorsement of an Approval Not Required (ANR) plan to incorporate land from an abutting parcel at 5 Winter Island Road into his existing yacht yard at 3A Winter Island Road, following the abutter's decision to sell. No new lots or structures are proposed.

events.project_name: 170 North Street — 8-Unit Residential Conversion
events.applicant: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC
events.summary: Noble Horizon Homes LLC proposes to create 8 residential units within an existing building at 170 North Street in the R2 and Entrance Corridor Overlay districts, with 16 parking spaces including an existing detached garage. The applicant did not appear and the site plan review hearing was continued to May 21, 2026.

contacts.name: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

events.project_name: 15 Crosby Street Definitive Subdivision
events.summary: 15 Crosby LLC received approval of a definitive subdivision plan to split one existing lot into two lots at 15 Crosby Street, including a waiver of frontage requirements for Lot B. Both the waivers and the decision passed 8-0 with one abstention, conditioned on updated contour and grade information being added to the civil plans prior to submission.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.project_name: 45 Jefferson Avenue Self-Storage Facility
events.summary: ZXE, LLC received site plan approval (9-0) to demolish existing buildings and construct a new four-story, approximately 100,000 sq ft self-storage facility with 735 units (721 interior, 14 exterior) at 45 Jefferson Avenue in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay districts. The approved decision incorporates conditions from the City Engineering Department tied to demolition, building permit, and certificate of occupancy milestones, along with a Design Review Board positive recommendation, Waiver of Demolition Delay, and Order of Conditions already in hand.

events.project_name: 50 Washington Street — Veterans Housing at Tabernacle Congregational Church
events.summary: Beacon Communities Services LLC proposes to renovate the Tabernacle Congregational Church building and add two new additions within existing parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans at 50 Washington Street in the B5 (Central Development) district, while retaining existing church uses in the building. The Site Plan Review and Planned Unit Development Special Permit hearing was continued to May 21, 2026.

events.project_name: 4 Franklin Street — Ambulance Garage and Office Amendment
events.summary: CAS Salem, LLC seeks to amend a 2021 site plan approval at 4 Franklin Street in the North River Canal Corridor, reducing office space from two stories to one story and extending the ambulance garage, with revised parking layout, lighting, landscaping, and utilities; a second phase on the North River side is deferred. The hearing was continued to May 7, 2026.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences Phase 1 — AvalonBay
events.address: 8 Harrison Road / 9 Harrison Road / 262 Loring Avenue / 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.summary: AvalonBay Communities received Smart Growth Overlay District plan approval (7-0, 2 abstentions) for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences: a 340-unit residential building with an above-level parking garage (524 spaces), two outdoor courtyards, and an approximately half-acre landscaped common on former Salem State University South Campus parcels, including extension of Harrison Road and a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead with five dedicated parking spaces.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences Phase 2 — WinnDevelopment
events.address: 8 Harrison Road / 9 Harrison Road / 262 Loring Avenue / 266 Loring Avenue, Salem, MA
events.summary: WinnDevelopment Companies proposes up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three former Salem State University South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus a new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces and landscaped pedestrian paths — the second phase of the Forest River Residences. The Smart Growth Overlay District plan review hearing was continued to May 7, 2026.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.project_name: BlueWave Community Solar Program
events.summary: City Council authorized the Mayor to execute 20-year contracts with BlueWave, a Boston-based solar developer, to deliver energy savings from solar projects to income-eligible residential customers in Salem through the city's municipal aggregation program. A revolving fund may also be established to collect BlueWave incentive payments and reimburse the current aggregation supplier, First Point Power, for discounts it extends to eligible residents.

events.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Road, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing on a Notice of Intent for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State South Campus site (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327). No unit count or square footage is stated in this agenda.

events.project_name: Lead Mills Seawall Repair
events.summary: Public hearing on a City of Salem Notice of Intent for in-kind seawall repairs and landside erosion restoration at the Lead Mills seawall on Lafayette St, a publicly owned coastal infrastructure site within a wetlands-protected area. No cost figures are stated.

events.project_name: North of Common Bed and Breakfast
events.summary: The ZBA approved a parking variance and special permit for Jeremy Schwartz and Shelby Soloff to operate a proposed six-guest-room, owner-occupied bed and breakfast at 25 Washington Square North. Two on-site parking spaces were approved where eight are required, subject to a special condition requiring documentation of at least three leased off-street spaces within a half mile during September–November 15. Interior work includes bathroom additions and removals, conversion of fireplaces to gas, and installation of sprinklers and fire suppression systems.

events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA
events.summary: 25 Bow Salem, LLC is seeking dimensional variance relief to reconfigure two nonconforming R1 lots—a 5,008 sq ft parcel on Bow Street and a 6,909 sq ft parcel on Ord Street (required minimum 15,000 sq ft)—along with a special permit for a second-story deck at 25 Bow Street within its north setback. A cease-and-desist was issued after the deck was constructed prior to ZBA approval; the board continued the petition to its May 6, 2026 special meeting for further review of Planning Board sequencing and abutter privacy concerns.

The ZBA approved a special permit for Justin Cook to demolish an existing nonconforming heavy industrial structure on a half-acre site at 33-37 Osborne Street and construct a new two-story building for personal automobile storage (capacity 51 vehicles, with car elevator) and second-floor business offices, with 16 exterior parking spaces and modest landscaping. No vehicle repair will occur on-site; the historic commission previously waived a demolition delay for the existing structure, and further Planning Board approval is required.

CarFive Motors LLC is seeking a special permit to add motor vehicle general repair and body work use to an existing automobile dealership at 296 Highland Avenue in the B2 / Entrance Corridor Overlay District. The matter was continued to the May 6, 2026 special meeting with no public comment received.

events.project_name: 102 Margin Street Rear Addition
events.summary: Continued petition at 102 Margin Street (R2 district) for Variances and a Special Permit to construct a rear addition and staircase encroaching into side and rear setbacks. Relief is also sought for lot area per dwelling unit (1,283 sq ft) and a parking reduction from 3 to 2 spaces, indicating a multi-unit residential property.

events.project_name: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street Lot Reconfiguration
events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA 01970
events.summary: Continued petition by 25 Bow Salem, LLC for Variance and Special Permit relief on two abutting R1 parcels with modified lot boundaries: a 5,008 sq ft Bow Street lot and a 6,909 sq ft Ord Street lot, both deficient in frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. The Special Permit would authorize a new second-story deck within Bow Street's north setback.

events.project_name: CarFive Motors 296 Highland Avenue Special Permit
events.summary: First hearing on a Special Permit for CarFive Motors LLC to add motor vehicle general repair and body work to an existing automobile dealership at 296 Highland Avenue in the B2/Entrance Corridor Overlay District. No structural changes to the building are described; the request addresses the use expansion only.

events.project_name: Ordinance #46 — Home Occupation Zoning Amendment
events.summary: The Committee on Ordinances, Licenses and Legal Affairs discussed a draft zoning ordinance amendment that would expand allowable home occupations in Salem, permitting residents to operate small businesses (visual arts, recording studios, hair styling, family day care, etc.) from their homes without a special permit, subject to standards for appearance, activity, noise, parking, and storage. The committee debated enforceability questions — including square-footage caps (20% vs. 25% of gross floor area), shipment limits, accessory structures, and prohibited uses — but did not reach consensus on draft language. The item was kept in committee 5-0 pending a future meeting with city department heads including the building inspector and city solicitor.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences Phase 2
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued joint public hearing before the Planning Board and Design Review Board for Smart Growth Overlay District Plan Review and C-ROD Site Plan Review. The applicant proposes up to 145 dwelling units through adaptive reuse of three existing Salem State University South Campus buildings and construction of one new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces and landscaped common areas. This is Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences project.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.project_name: Forest River Residences Phase 1
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing for Smart Growth Overlay District Plan Review and C-ROD Site Plan Review for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences. The applicant proposes a 340-unit residential building with an above-grade parking garage (524 spaces), two enclosed outdoor courtyards, an approximately half-acre landscaped common, an extension of Harrison Road, and a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences Phase 2 (WinnDevelopment)
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, and 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Joint public hearing closed 8-0 by both the Planning Board and Design Review Board for Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences on the former Salem State University South Campus. WinnDevelopment proposes up to 145 senior housing units through adaptive reuse of three historic campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) and construction of one new four-story building along Harrison Road, with 159 surface parking spaces. The project will receive historic tax credits and is coordinated with the adjacent AvalonBay Phase 1; no final approval vote was taken at this session.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.project_name: 15 Crosby Street Definitive Subdivision
events.summary: Continued public hearing for a definitive subdivision to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of frontage requirements for Lot B. Updated stormwater, grading, and sewer connection plans were presented; board cannot approve without complete contour plans, and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.project_name: 45 Jefferson Avenue Self-Storage Facility
events.summary: Continued public hearing for demolition of existing buildings at 45 Jefferson Avenue and construction of a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior) and 11 parking spaces in the Industrial, Coastal Resiliency Overlay, and Entrance Corridor Overlay Districts. Item was continued to April 23, 2026 at the applicant's request without substantive board discussion.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences Phase 1 (AvalonBay)
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, and 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued public hearing for Phase 1 of the Forest River Residences, with AvalonBay proposing a 340-unit residential building, a 524-space above-grade parking garage, and a half-acre central landscaped common on the former Salem State University campus. Updated plans address 2070 flood elevation analysis, accessible pathway grading, raised crosswalk treatments, and building signage; continued to April 23, 2026.

events.project_name: 4 Franklin Street Site Plan Amendment
events.summary: Public hearing for an amendment to a 2021 Site Plan Review, Flood Hazard Special Permit, and North River Canal Corridor Special Permit decision at 4 Franklin Street, reducing office space from two stories to one story and extending an ambulance garage. Updated building facade with simplified colors and trellis elements, HVAC relocated to rooftop, and revised landscaping and utilities were presented; engineering peer review is still pending and the item was continued to April 23, 2026.

events.project_name: 170 North Street Site Plan Review
events.applicant: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC
events.summary: Public hearing for site plan review to legalize eight existing residential units at 170 North Street following a ZBA variance; the building has operated with eight units since 1999 despite being previously permitted for five, including a noncompliant basement unit. Proposed work includes adding an egress well to bring the basement unit into compliance and installing a new fire protection line; continued to April 23, 2026 pending engineering peer review and Design Review Board referral.

contacts.name: Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC

events.project_name: Cross Avenue Utility Pole Relocation
events.summary: The City Council approved relocation of one jointly owned National Grid/Verizon utility pole on Cross Avenue, closing a continued public hearing. The approval was conditioned on the relocated pole carrying secondary transmission lines only — no primary lines or transformers — and included an informal request to the Tree Commission to replace a mature cherry tree displaced by the work.

events.project_name: 50 Washington Street — Veterans Housing (Tabernacle Congregational Church)
events.summary: Beacon Communities Services LLC seeks Site Plan Review and a Planned Unit Development Special Permit to renovate the existing Tabernacle Congregational Church building and construct two additions within existing parking lots to create 48 studio apartments for veterans. Nine car-parking spaces and utility and landscaping improvements are also proposed; existing church uses will be retained. Continued to the April 23, 2026 meeting on a 7-0 vote.

events.project_name: 45 Jefferson Avenue Self-Storage Facility
events.summary: ZXE LLC proposes to demolish existing buildings at 45 Jefferson Avenue and construct a new four-story, approximately 100,000-square-foot self-storage facility with 735 storage units (721 interior, 14 exterior-accessible) and 11 parking spaces. The project has received Design Review Board approval; one peer reviewer is expected to submit a final response to the city the following week, with a vote targeted for the April 9, 2026 meeting.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences — Phase 1 (AvalonBay)
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: AvalonBay Communities proposes Phase 1 of the two-phase Forest River Residences: a new residential building with 340 units, an above-grade parking garage with 524 spaces, two enclosed outdoor courtyards, a half-acre landscaped common, and an extension of Harrison Road with a connection to the Forest River Conservation Trailhead. The project has Design Review Board approval; peer review is expected to conclude before the April 9, 2026 hearing where a vote is anticipated, with the 120-day clock expiring May 7.

events.project_name: Forest River Residences — Phase 2 (WinnDevelopment)
events.address: 8 Harrison Road, 9 Harrison Road, 262 Loring Ave, 266 Loring Ave, Salem, MA
events.summary: WinnDevelopment proposes Phase 2 of the Forest River Residences: adaptive reuse of three former Salem State University South Campus buildings (Harrington, Academic, and Alumni) plus one new four-story residential building along Harrison Road, for a total of up to 145 dwelling units, 159 surface parking spaces, and landscaped pedestrian common areas. A joint traffic study covering both phases was presented, projecting approximately 160–165 peak-hour vehicle trips; the matter was continued to April 9, 2026, with the 120-day clock expiring June 4.

contacts.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC

events.project_name: Haunted Warren Museum Extended Hours Petition
events.summary: The City Council approved extended operating hours for the Haunted Warren Museum, a proposed ticketed indoor paranormal-education experience at 259 Essex Street, on a 90-day trial basis commencing upon issuance of a certificate of occupancy. Approved hours are 6 AM–10 PM Sunday through Thursday and 6 AM–midnight Friday and Saturday, with a follow-up hearing scheduled for September 10, 2026 to reassess.

events.project_name: National Grid Conduit Installation – Lafayette St. & Savoy Rd.
events.address: Intersection of Lafayette St. and Savoy Rd., Salem, MA
events.summary: National Grid received City Council approval to install underground conduits at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Savoy Road to provide new electrical service to Salem State University at 354 Lafayette Street.

events.project_name: National Grid & Verizon Joint Pole Installation – Lafayette St. & Fairview Rd.
events.address: Intersection of Lafayette St. and Fairview Rd., Salem, MA
events.summary: National Grid and Verizon received City Council approval to install one jointly owned utility pole at the intersection of Lafayette Street and Fairview Road to support underground electrical work at Salem State University.

events.project_name: National Grid & Verizon Joint Pole Relocation – Cross Ave.
events.summary: National Grid and Verizon's petition to relocate one jointly owned utility pole on Cross Avenue was continued to the April 9, 2026 City Council meeting at the applicant's request in order to first meet with the Ward Councillor.

events.project_name: Mansell Field Remediation & Restoration Project
events.summary: The City Council appropriated $60,000 from the Capital Outlay account for installation of an underdrain to complete the Mansell Field Remediation & Restoration Project, a public park that has been fenced off since 2020 due to flooding and overgrowth. The project returns the park to public use.

events.project_name: Definitive Subdivision Plan of 15 Crosby Street
events.applicant: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC
events.summary: Continuance of a public hearing for a Definitive Subdivision Plan to split one existing lot at 15 Crosby Street into two lots, with a requested waiver of the Subdivision Control Law frontage requirement for Lot B.

contacts.name: Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

events.project_name: 14-16 Bridge Street Lot Split
events.summary: ZBA approved a variance to re-split a merged lot at 14-16 Bridge Street into two nonconforming lots: a 2,982-sq-ft lot at 14 Bridge Street (41.25-ft frontage) and a 4,094-sq-ft lot at 16 Bridge Street (50-ft frontage), both below the 60-ft minimum. The petitioner plans to construct a new two-unit residence on the newly created lot; additional Planning Board relief will be required before construction.

events.project_name: 99 Leach Street 3.5-Story Two-Family House
events.summary: ZBA denied a variance to construct a 3.5-story two-family house on a nonconforming lot in the Coastal Resiliency Overlay District. The proposal sought relief for lot area per dwelling unit (2,222 sq ft vs. 7,500 required), building height (3.5 vs. 2.5 stories), and rear setback (23 ft vs. 30 ft required); the motion failed 3-2, with opponents arguing self-created hardship, flood risk, and dimensional inconsistencies in the submitted plans.

events.project_name: 74 Beaver Street Lot Split and Two-Family Townhouse
events.summary: ZBA approved a variance to subdivide 74 Beaver Street into two nonconforming lots — a 4,502-sq-ft parcel along Beaver Street and a 5,621-sq-ft parcel along Silver Street — and to construct a new three-bedroom two-family townhouse with a 3-ft front setback and 12-ft rear setback. A condition requires a revised plot plan showing three code-compliant parking spaces before a building permit is issued.

events.project_name: 25 Washington Square North Bed and Breakfast
events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to operate a bed and breakfast at 25 Washington Square North. The applicants seek a parking variance (2 on-site spaces vs. 8 required, with 6 off-site spaces within a quarter mile) and a special permit for the B&B use; no exterior structural changes are proposed.

events.project_name: 102 Margin Street Two-Family Conversion
events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to legalize a de-facto two-family use at 102 Margin Street by adding a rear exterior staircase and converting an existing rear porch to a bedroom, with no footprint increase. The Board requested dimensioned plans with parking layout, a statement of hardship, and clarification on the full extent of zoning relief needed, including potential parking relief.

events.project_name: 25 Bow Street / 32 Ord Street Lot Boundary Adjustment
events.address: 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, Salem, MA
events.summary: Petition continued to April 15, 2026 to adjust lot boundaries between 25 Bow Street and 32 Ord Street, yielding two nonconforming residential lots (5,008 sq ft and 6,909 sq ft) with deficient frontage, width, and lot area per dwelling unit. The petition also includes a special permit for a new second-story deck within the north setback of 25 Bow Street; an immediate abutter objected to the deck's privacy impact.

events.project_name: 33-37 Osborne Street Auto Storage and Office Redevelopment
events.summary: Special permit petition continued to April 15, 2026 to replace a nonconforming heavy industrial use at 33-37 Osborne Street with a new two-story building featuring first-floor automobile storage and second-floor business offices. The proposal involves clearing the entire existing site and constructing a new structure that complies with dimensional and parking requirements.

events.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Rd, Salem, MA
events.summary: Continued Conservation Commission public hearing on Notice of Intent (DEP #64-825) filed by AvalonBay Communities Inc for demolition of an existing building and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements at the Salem State South Campus site (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327). Site is within a wetland-protected area under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act and Salem's local ordinance.

entities.name: Joseph C. Correnti, Esq. f/b/o WinnDevelopment Companies, LLC
Scott Grover, Esq. F/b/o North Shore Community Development Corporation (NSCDC) and North Shore Community Health Center (NSCHC)
House of the Seven Gables Settlement Association
Jordan Ryan D/B/A Noble Horizon Homes LLC
Scott Grover, Esq. f/b/o 15 Crosby LLC

upcoming.project_name: 26-69-WB Website Hosting & Digital Marketing Services for the North Shore Workforce Board
upcoming.summary: City of Salem Purchasing Department is soliciting open-market bids for website hosting and digital marketing services on behalf of the North Shore Workforce Board. The bid closing date was extended from June 16 to June 23, 2026 via Addendum 1. Bid documents (BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-130060) are available on CommBuys.

upcoming.project_name: 11 Vinnin Street – Change of Use to Retail/Gourmet Shop
upcoming.summary: Turnstone II, LLC seeks a Special Permit to convert a nonconforming medical office to a nonconforming retail and gourmet shop with on-site baked goods and food preparation. No construction scope or square footage is stated in the agenda.
Kayla Dorst seeks a Finding to add an approximately 25-by-22-foot accessory dwelling unit to a nonconforming single-family house, with a proposed 13.3-foot front setback in the R1 district.

upcoming.project_name: 278 Lafayette Street – Two Additional Dwelling Units
upcoming.summary: Robert J. Willwerth III seeks a Variance to add two dwelling units to a property that already contains six, reducing lot area per unit from 3,683 sq ft to 2,762 sq ft where 3,500 sq ft per unit is required in the R3/Entrance Corridor Overlay District.

upcoming.project_name: 6 Lawrence Street – Additions to Nonconforming Three-Family House
upcoming.summary: Paul Caron seeks a Special Permit to add a two-story front porch and staircase (approximately 4'4" front setback) and a two-story rear porch and staircase (approximately 2'8" side setback) to a nonconforming three-family house in the R2/Coastal Resiliency Overlay District. No new units are proposed.

upcoming.project_name: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Road Multi-Family Development
upcoming.address: 262C Loring Ave & 8 Harrison Road, Salem, MA
upcoming.summary: Continuation of a public hearing on a Notice of Intent filed by AvalonBay Communities Inc for demolition of an existing building at the Salem State South Campus and construction of new multi-family residential buildings with associated infrastructure and stormwater improvements. The site encompasses parcels at 262C Loring Ave and 8 Harrison Road (Map 31, Lots 1 & 327) within wetlands jurisdiction under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act and Salem Wetlands Protection and Conservation Ordinance.

Continuation of a public hearing on a Request for Determination of Applicability by the City of Salem Engineering Department for shoreline protection improvements at Bay View Ave Beach. Work includes replacing the existing sidewalk along Bay View Ave, converting the stone beach access ramp to revetment stones to deter erosion, and adding rip-rap stone reinforcement behind the existing seawall.

Public hearing on a Notice of Intent filed by the Salem Harbor Port Authority to demolish the existing ferry terminal building at 10 Blaney Street and construct a new ferry terminal building relocated outside the FEMA VE flood zone. The site (Map 41, Lot 278) is within wetlands jurisdiction under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act and Salem Wetlands Protection and Conservation Ordinance.

upcoming.address: Salem Street between Chase Street and Lafayette Street, Salem, MA
upcoming.summary: Public hearing on a Notice of Intent filed by the City of Salem Transportation Department for roadway improvements, pedestrian accommodations, and stormwater infrastructure along a segment of Salem Street between Chase Street and Lafayette Street. The work corridor falls within areas subject to the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act and the Salem Wetlands Protection and Conservation Ordinance.

upcoming.project_name: 26-65-SPS Lease at Horace Mann Laboratory School for Early Education
upcoming.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids for a lease of space at Horace Mann Laboratory School to house an Early Education Program. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; bids are due June 16, 2026. Bid documents are available as '26-65-SPS RFP Manual - Lease at HMLS for ECC.pdf' via CommBuys (BD-23-1165-COSPS-COSPS-129373).

upcoming.project_name: 26-64-SPS Lease at Salem High School for Child Care
upcoming.summary: The City of Salem is soliciting proposals to lease space at Salem High School for a child care facility. This is an open-market, non-statewide solicitation (CommBuys BD-23-1165-COSPD-COS01-129370); a pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026. Bid documents are available via the RFP manual attached to the CommBuys posting.

upcoming.project_name: 26-63-SPS Lease at Collins Middle School for Youth Outreach
upcoming.summary: The City of Salem School Department is soliciting bids to lease space at Collins Middle School for a Youth Outreach Program. No square footage or lease value is stated. A pre-bid conference was held June 6, 2026; the RFP manual is available as an attachment on CommBuys.
